title: Touring the Campus
learningGoal: The learner will read a short descriptive paragraph utilizing Ko-So-A-Do demonstratives and the 'mo' particle to identify objects and places.
instructions: Read the short paragraph written by a university guide showing a new student around the campus. Pay attention to the demonstrative words (This, That, Here, There) to understand the physical layout of the tour.

contentItems.text: Remember that 'Koko' means here, and 'Asoko' means over there. 'Watashi no' means 'my'.

readingText: ここは だいがくです。あそこは カフェです。それは わたしの ほんです。これも わたしの ほんです。あの くるまは ジョンさんの くるまです。
textInTargetLanguage: ここは だいがくです。あそこは カフェです。それは わたしの ほんです。これも わたしの ほんです。あの くるまは ジョンさんの くるまです。
baseLanguageSupportText: Here is the university. Over there is the cafe. That (near you) is my book. This is ALSO my book. That car over there is John's car.

exampleSentence.targetText: ここは だいがくです。
あそこは カフェです。
これも わたしの ほんです。
exampleSentence.transliteration: Kore mo watashi no hon desu.

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: ここは だいがくです。あそこは カフェです。
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Koko wa daigaku desu. Asoko wa kafe desu.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: Here is the university. Over there is the cafe.

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: それは わたしの ほんです。これも わたしの ほんです。
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Sore wa watashi no hon desu. Kore mo watashi no hon desu.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: That is my book. This is also my book.

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: あの くるまは ジョンさんの くるまです。
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: Ano kuruma wa Jon-san no kuruma desu.
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: That car over there is John's car.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Where is the cafe located relative to the speaker?
Who does the 'Sore' (That) book belong to?
How does the speaker say 'This is ALSO my book'?
options: Kore wa watashi no hon desu.
Kore mo watashi no hon desu.
Sore mo watashi no hon desu.
Are mo watashi no hon desu.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Who does the car over there belong to?
Which adjective demonstrative is used to point to the car?

correctAnswers.q003: Kore mo watashi no hon desu.

expectedSummaryPoints: The speaker points out the university here and the cafe over there.
The speaker claims ownership of two books (that one and this one too).
The speaker points out John's car over there.

feedbackRules.onPass: Great reading! You successfully interpreted the distance of the objects.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the Ko-So-A-Do rules. 'Koko' is here, 'Soko' is there, 'Asoko' is over there.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Almost perfect! Remember that 'Kore mo' means 'This also'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: ここは だいがくです。あそこは カフェです。それは わたしの ほんです。これも わたしの ほんです。あの くるまは ジョンさんの くるまです。

hintText: Look closely at the particle 'mo'. It completely replaces 'wa' to mean 'also'.
commonMistakes: Thinking 'Ano kuruma' means 'This car'. 'Ano' is for things far away.
